extra.reasoning: Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only one rule sets rec_drug_a: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the full 10-way conjunction C = peptic_ulcer AND ckd_dialysis AND elderly AND asthma AND bradycardia AND prior_gi_bleed AND seizure_history AND immunosuppressed AND hyperkalemia AND pregnancy. So rec_drug_a = true implies C = true; otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. For rec_drug_b there are two rules: guard_suppress_b (priority 90) sets it false under the exact same conjunction C, and rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets it true under peptic_ulcer. When C is true, both b-rules match, but conflict resolution is by priority per output and 90 > 50, so guard_suppress_b wins and rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a can be true (C true), the higher-priority guard forces rec_drug_b false. When C is false, rec_drug_a is false (default), so the conjunction is false regardless of b. There is no other rule writing rec_drug_a, and no way for both to be true simultaneously. The rec_drug_c rules are irrelevant to this property. The property holds over the entire input space.
